violations.text: The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelves have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

The floors in the walk-in cooler, ice cream freezer, and soda storage room area have an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/04/2026

The door to the outside, located in the main entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Frozen pizzas were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The 3-bay sink faucet was observed leaking and in poor repair. The right side of the open air case was observed holding an ambient temperature of 45 DF. No TCS food is to be stored on the right side of unit until it is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025

Establishment is lacking proper drain plugs for the 3-bay sink. Only one proper drain plug was observed at the time of inspection. Establishment is to obtain drain plugs. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelving have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The floor in the walk-in cooler area has an accumulation of soil residue and food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 12/08/2025

Various food was observed held between 48-58 degrees Fahrenheit on the right side of grab n go unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. All food on right side of unit was evaluated and voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection (see attachment). Food on left side of cooler was observed between 41-44 DF. All food on left side of unit was moved to walk-in cooler at the time of inspection. Establishment is not to store any TCS foods in unit until unit is able to hold an ambient temperature of 41 DF or less. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The door to the outside, located in the main entrance, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Frozen pizzas were observed stored on the floor in the walk-in freezer. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The lowboy freezer handle was observed with tape and in poor repair. The 3-bay sink faucet was observed leaking and in poor repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 11/26/2025
Establishment is lacking proper drain plugs for the 3-bay sink. Establishment is to obtain drain plugs. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the walk-in cooler shelving have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 11/19/2025
